front: What does the `is_active` flag control in a VIP agent?
back: When UVM_ACTIVE, the agent builds driver + sequencer + monitor. When UVM_PASSIVE, only the monitor and coverage collector are built — the agent observes without driving.

front: Why should protocol assertions be placed in the SV interface rather than the testbench?
back: Interface assertions fire continuously on every clock edge regardless of test scenario, catching protocol violations even when no test-specific checks are active.

front: What is the purpose of a VIP package file (`_pkg.sv`)?
back: It controls compilation order (leaf components before containers) and provides a clean namespace, serving as the single entry point for importing the VIP into any testbench.
